checks.a_analysis: The output contains no fabricated paragraphs or sentences. The document title in metadata is AI-generated (which is allowed) and differs slightly from the header, but all body content in the output sections has a basis in the source document.
Every phone number, meeting ID, and street address present in the output correctly matches the data in the source document. No invented contact information was found.
Both pages are present in the output. All primary numbered agenda sections (1 through 13) are represented, though many have their internal contents truncated.

The table on page 1 of the source document contains 7 total rows (1 header and 6 data rows). The JSON output correctly contains exactly 7 rows in its table structure. While cell alignment is incorrect in the first two data rows, the number of rows is consistent.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The document has suffered substantial content loss during conversion: 1) In Section 4, the financial details for items b, c, d, and e (fund balances and disbursements) are entirely missing. 2) Item 4g is missing its entire sub-list of five peer reviewer names. 3) Item 5 is missing its entire sub-list of six report topics (a through f). 4) The header is missing the '20190111 NC 911 Board WebEx Link'. 5) In the Page 1 table, rows 1 and 2 are missing their final data cells, causing the presenter information ('Eric Boyette' and 'Pokey Harris') to be incorrectly shifted into the 'Topic' column. Collectively, these omissions account for over 30% of the source document's information.

checks.b_evidence_location: Sections 4 and 5, Page 1 header, Page 1 Table (reading_order 5, 6, 10, 11)

checks.a_analysis: The text that was successfully captured matches the source document with no significant wording changes that alter the meaning of the preserved sections.

checks.a_analysis: The content from the source PDF appears only once in the output JSON. There are no duplicated paragraphs or sections.
The heading hierarchy is logical and follows a consistent structure (H1 for the document title, H2 for major sections and meeting titles, H3 for the logistics note). No levels are skipped.

The table structure is corrupted in two ways. First, the data rows for 'Call to Order' and 'Roll Call' only contain two cells, which misaligns them with the three-column header (Tab, Topic, Presenter). Second, the agenda is visually a continuous table/aligned list, but the output truncates the table after item 4 and converts the rest into fragmented lists and paragraphs, losing the structural unity of the agenda.
checks.b_evidence_source: The agenda items (Call to Order, Roll Call, 1-13) are presented in a structured layout with columns for Tab, Topic, and Presenter.
checks.b_evidence_output: The output table (element index 5) only includes items up to item 4. Data rows like 'Call to Order' only have 2 cells despite a 3-cell header.

checks.a_analysis: The reading order for items 5 through 13 on page 2 is significantly broken. The output groups the agenda topics into one list (reading order 13) and then lists the presenters as individual paragraphs (reading order 14-20) at the end of the section. This separates the topics from their respective presenters, making the document logically incoherent as a reading sequence.
checks.b_evidence_source: Agenda item 6 'Education Committee Report' is horizontally aligned with 'Mike Reitz'. Item 7 'Funding Committee Report' is aligned with 'David Bone'.
checks.b_evidence_output: Reading order 13: List containing '6. Education Committee Report', '7. Funding Committee Report'... then Reading order 14: 'Mike Reitz', Reading order 15: 'David Bone'.

checks.a_analysis: The list structure is failing in two critical areas. First, the nested sub-list under item 4g (items i through v, naming peer reviewers like Vicki Callicutt) is entirely missing from the output. Second, the detailed financial rows under items 4b, 4c, 4d, and 4e (e.g., 'November 2018 Fund Balance $27,867,858') are omitted, reducing complex informational list items to simple headers.
checks.b_evidence_source: g) Peer Reviewer Approvals... i. Vicki Callicutt... ii. Megan Daub... AND b) NG 911 Fund [Financial details follows]
checks.b_evidence_output: The list items for g) and b-e) contain only the primary line text with no nested items or detail lines included.

checks.a_analysis: A clear external link present in the source PDF header ('20190111 NC 911 Board WebEx Link') is missing from the output JSON content.

checks.a_analysis: The visual organization of the document is significantly compromised. First, the association between agenda items and their presenters is lost from item 6 onwards. In the source, presenters are aligned horizontally with the topics; in the HTML, they are moved into a separate sequential list at the bottom of the document, making it impossible to map topics to presenters without manually counting. Second, the detailed financial data (fund balances and disbursements) listed under agenda items 4b, 4c, 4d, and 4e in the source is completely missing from the rendered HTML output. Third, a link in the header ('20190111 NC 911 Board WebEx Link') present in the source is missing from the HTML header.
checks.b_evidence_source: In the source PDF (Page 1 and 2), items 4b-4e contain detailed financial lines (e.g., 'November 2018 Fund Balance $27,867,858'), and items 6-12 have presenters aligned to the right (e.g., '6. Education Committee Report' next to 'Mike Reitz').
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML screenshot shows items 4b-4e as single bullet points with no financial data below them. Items 6-12 are listed as bullets, and the presenter names ('Mike Reitz', 'David Bone', etc.) appear in a separate list much further down the page.

checks.a_analysis: The agenda table is visually corrupted. For the first two rows ('Call to Order' and 'Roll Call'), the data is misaligned across columns: the topic name is placed in the 'Tab' column and the presenter's name is placed in the 'Topic' column. Furthermore, the table is truncated; while the source maintains a consistent tabular layout for the entire agenda, the HTML renders everything after item 4 as plain text or bulleted lists, breaking the structural integrity of the document.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source PDF (Page 1) shows 'Call to Order' in the Topic column and 'Eric Boyette' in the Presenter column.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML screenshot shows 'Call to Order' in the Tab column and 'Eric Boyette' in the Topic column.

checks.a_analysis: The primary meaningful image from the source, the 'NC DIT 911 Board' logo, is successfully rendered in the HTML document with its graphical elements intact.

There is no evidence of body content (paragraphs, tables) being duplicated in the HTML output. The repetition of headings in the Table of Contents and the body is a standard accessibility feature and does not constitute a defect.
